Name: Archaeological Cultural Landscape of Carnuntum
Location: Lower Austria, between Petronell-Carnuntum and Bad Deutsch-Altenburg, Danube Limes
Values_List: Historical significance (Roman frontier / Danube Limes); Scientific value (vast unexcavated areas); General cultural significance (visible monuments, tourism); Landscape value (integrated archaeological landscape)
Assessment_Method: Austrian Federal Monuments Authority criteria: historical, artistic, general cultural significance
Threats_List: Development pressure; Agricultural activity; Infrastructure projects
Integrity_Notes: High integrity due to 90%+ remaining unexcavated. Agricultural use has been largely non-destructive. Some areas affected by historical stone robbing.
Comparators: Other Danube Limes sites; comparable to Vindolanda (UK), Xanten (Germany)
Unique_Features: One of the best-preserved Roman frontier landscapes in Europe; extensive use of non-invasive prospection technologies

Name: Alpine Landscape at Pitschenbergalm
Location: Upper Austria, Alpine region, mountain pasture at high altitude
Values_List: Historical significance (long-term alpine land use); Scientific value (multi-period stratification); General cultural significance (traditional alpine pastoral culture); Landscape value (intact alpine setting)
Assessment_Method: Austrian Federal Monuments Authority: historical significance + general cultural significance evaluation
Threats_List: Climate change effects on alpine environments; Changes in pastoral land use; Forestry activity
Integrity_Notes: Relatively well preserved due to remote alpine location. Minimal modern disturbance.
Comparators: Other alpine archaeological sites in Austria and Switzerland
Unique_Features: Demonstrates significance assessment of landscape-scale alpine archaeology; LiDAR discovery

Name: Archaeological Landscape in Kurese
Values_List: Historical significance (continuous settlement); Scientific value (multi-period landscape); Cultural landscape value (field systems, land use patterns)
Assessment_Method: Estonian Heritage Conservation Act criteria; National Heritage Board evaluation
Threats_List: Forest management; Metal detecting; Agricultural intensification; Lengthy protection process
Integrity_Notes: Variable - some areas well preserved under forest, others affected by modern agriculture
Unique_Features: Illustrates tension between heritage protection and forest/land management in Baltic context

Name: Mapping Archaeologically-sensitive Areas for Municipalities
Values_List: Administrative value (planning tool); Scientific value (predictive modeling); Preventive value (development guidance)
Assessment_Method: Predictive GIS-based modeling combined with known site data
Threats_List: Urban development without archaeological consideration; Lack of archaeological data in planning
Comparators: Similar GIS-based predictive approaches in Netherlands, UK, Scandinavia
Unique_Features: Proactive approach to heritage management through spatial planning integration; landscape-scale assessment

Name: Raseborg Castle
Values_List: Cultural-historic significance (medieval power center); Scientific value (architecture, stratification); Landscape value (coastal setting); Educational value (accessible ruin, tourism)
Assessment_Method: Finnish Heritage Agency scoring system: cultural-historic significance, representativeness, rarity, information value, landscape/environmental value. Scale 0-2 or 1-3.
Threats_List: Natural deterioration of ruins; Tourism pressure; Vegetation growth
Integrity_Notes: Castle ruins in variable condition. Scored high (3) on cultural-historic significance.
Unique_Features: Demonstrates Finnish quantitative scoring approach to significance assessment

Name: The Vrouw Maria Wreck
Location: Archipelago National Park, southwestern Finland, Baltic Sea
Values_List: Cultural-historic significance (international trade, art history); Scientific value (maritime archaeology, ship construction); Historical significance (Catherine the Great connection); Symbolic value (international cultural relations)
Assessment_Method: Finnish three-level classification for wrecks; multi-criteria evaluation including cultural-historic significance, representativeness, rarity, information potential
Threats_List: Unauthorized diving; Environmental degradation; Salvage attempts
Integrity_Notes: Exceptionally well preserved in cold Baltic conditions. Cargo potentially intact.
Unique_Features: Demonstrates underwater heritage significance assessment; intersection of art history and maritime archaeology

Name: Protection of Tumuli in Hungary
Values_List: Historical significance (multiple cultures and periods); Landscape value (defining feature of Hungarian plains); Scientific value (unexcavated archaeological potential); Symbolic value (connection to steppe cultures)
Assessment_Method: Hungarian heritage protection criteria; inventory and mapping
Threats_List: Agricultural plowing; Infrastructure development; Erosion; Illegal excavation
Integrity_Notes: Highly variable - many reduced by agriculture; some well preserved in pastureland
Comparators: Kurgans in Ukraine, Russia; barrows in UK, Netherlands, Scandinavia
Unique_Features: Collection-level management challenge; landscape-scale heritage protection; agricultural landscape context

Name: Pilis Mountains and Szentendre Island Programme
Location: Pilis Mountains and Szentendre Island, near Budapest, Hungary
Values_List: Historical significance (medieval royal centers); Scientific value (systematic landscape survey); Cultural landscape value (multi-period occupation); Educational value (university research involvement)
Assessment_Method: Regional systematic survey with GIS; multi-criteria landscape assessment
Threats_List: Urban sprawl from Budapest; Forestry; Tourism pressure; Quarrying
Integrity_Notes: Variable across the region. Forest cover has protected some areas; others affected by modern development.
Comparators: Other regional landscape programmes in Europe
Unique_Features: Largest case study (35K chars); demonstrates regional-scale systematic approach; academic-management partnership

Name: Hveravellir
Location: Northern Iceland, highland interior between Langjökull and Hofsjökull glaciers
Values_List: Historical significance (medieval travel routes, saga connections); Scientific value (archaeological remains in extreme environment); Natural heritage value (geothermal features); Intangible heritage value (saga traditions, outlaw stories); Landscape value (highland wilderness)
Assessment_Method: Icelandic scoring system with 10 evaluation areas; Cultural Heritage Agency of Iceland evaluation
Threats_List: Tourism pressure (geothermal bathing); Climate change; Infrastructure development; Volcanic activity
Integrity_Notes: Some remains well preserved in highland conditions. Tourism infrastructure has impacted some areas.
Comparators: Other Icelandic highland sites; comparable geothermal heritage sites globally
Unique_Features: Nature-culture interface; extreme environment archaeology; saga tradition connections; outlaw heritage

Name: Þjórsárdalur Cultural Landscape
Values_List: Historical significance (Viking settlement); Scientific value (tephra-sealed archaeological deposits); Landscape value (volcanic landscape, abandoned settlement pattern); Educational value (Stöng reconstruction); Intangible value (connection to saga period)
Assessment_Method: Cultural landscape significance assessment by Cultural Heritage Agency of Iceland
Threats_List: Erosion; Hydropower development; Tourism; River course changes
Integrity_Notes: Exceptional preservation under volcanic tephra. Some areas disturbed by river activity and erosion.
Comparators: Pompeii (volcanic preservation); other Icelandic settlement sites
Unique_Features: Volcanic preservation creating "frozen in time" moment; connection between natural disaster and cultural heritage

Name: Waterford City Viking Triangle
Values_List: Historical significance (oldest Irish city, Viking foundation); Archaeological value (extensive stratified deposits); Urban heritage value (living historic city); Social value (community identity, tourism); Educational value (museum and interpretation)
Assessment_Method: Irish National Monuments legislation; archaeological significance criteria within development control
Threats_List: Urban development pressure; Infrastructure modernization; Flood risk
Integrity_Notes: Deeply stratified urban deposits. Some areas excavated; extensive deposits remain in situ beneath modern city.
Unique_Features: Successful integration of archaeology into urban regeneration; development-led archaeology in living city

Name: Tuba-Zangariyye Dolmen Field
Location: Korazim Plateau, northern Israel, between Sea of Galilee and Hula Valley
Values_List: Historical significance (4,500-year-old burial tradition); Scientific value (megalithic construction, unexcavated potential); Landscape value (megalithic cultural landscape); Rarity (one of last remaining intact dolmen concentrations); Biblical/intangible value (possible references in Deuteronomy and Mark)
Assessment_Method: Context-Based Significance Assessment (CBSA) model (Alef, 2021). Five-step process: identify elements/contexts → articulate significance → contextualize → compare → summarize.
Threats_List: Residential development (Ministry of Housing); Infrastructure construction; Basalt mining; Historical salvage-excavation policy
Integrity_Notes: Well-preserved concentration, though dozens of dolmens destroyed in wider Korazim Plateau. Some covering stones weigh up to 15 tons.
Comparators: Korazim Plateau wider dolmen field; Golan Heights megalithic sites; southern Syria megalithic field
Unique_Features: Demonstrates CBSA methodology in practice; shift from salvage to preservation; biblical landscape connections

Name: Leithon
Location: Leiderdorp, near The Hague and Leiden, western Netherlands
Values_List: Scientific value (early medieval settlement patterns); Historical significance (Merovingian/Carolingian occupation); Rarity (well-preserved early medieval site in urban context)
Assessment_Method: Dutch rating system: intrinsic (scientific) value, physical quality, absolute quality. National scheduling criteria.
Threats_List: Urban development (densely populated area); Infrastructure works; Water table changes
Integrity_Notes: Archaeological deposits preserved beneath modern surface (tennis courts). Depth provides some protection.
Comparators: Other Rhine delta early medieval settlements; Dorestad
Unique_Features: Demonstrates Dutch intrinsic-value-based assessment system; preservation under modern urban surface

Name: Prehistoric Barrows as Archive for Future Research
Values_List: Scientific value (future research potential); Historical significance (prehistoric burial tradition); Landscape value (visible heritage features); Archival value (information stored for future retrieval)
Assessment_Method: Future research value assessment; Dutch heritage management framework
Threats_List: Agriculture; Development; Excavation that removes future research potential; Erosion
Integrity_Notes: Variable. Many barrows leveled by agriculture; surviving examples increasingly rare.
Comparators: UK round barrows; Scandinavian burial mounds; Hungarian tumuli (case 7.7)
Unique_Features: Explicitly frames archaeological sites as archives for future research; challenges excavation-first approach

Name: Hillfort Zamczysko (The Burgstall)
Values_List: Historical significance (early medieval fortification); Scientific value (preserved stratification); Landscape value (prominent topographic feature); Representativeness (type-site for early medieval hillforts)
Assessment_Method: Polish National Institute of Cultural Heritage criteria; Voivodship Monuments Preservation Officer evaluation
Threats_List: Forest growth affecting ramparts; Illegal metal detecting; Erosion; Neglect
Integrity_Notes: Rampart structure preserved. Some disturbance from historical and illegal activities.
Comparators: Other early medieval hillforts in Poland and Central Europe
Unique_Features: Demonstrates Polish heritage protection system; landscape-prominent archaeological feature

Name: Church of St Nicholas and the Romanesque Cellar, Gdańsk
Site_Type: Urban religious / architectural site with buried remains
Values_List: Historical significance (Gdańsk medieval urban development); Architectural value (Romanesque cellar, Gothic church); Scientific value (urban stratification); Symbolic value (Gdańsk identity, post-war reconstruction context)
Assessment_Method: Polish heritage protection criteria; architectural and archaeological combined assessment
Threats_List: Urban development; Vibration from traffic; Water table changes; Deterioration of below-ground remains
Integrity_Notes: Romanesque cellar well preserved beneath later construction. Urban context both protects and threatens.
Comparators: Other Hanseatic city archaeological sites; York, Lübeck
Unique_Features: Above-ground + below-ground heritage interplay; post-war urban reconstruction context

Name: Dumbarton Rock
Location: Dumbarton, west-central Scotland, confluence of River Leven and Clyde
Values_List: Historical significance (Alt Clut, medieval fortress); Natural heritage value (volcanic geology); Social value (rock climbing community); Intangible heritage (community connections); Landscape/scenic value (dramatic volcanic rock on river)
Assessment_Method: Historic Environment Scotland Statement of Significance format: chronological summary, thematic discussions, social overview, assessment of significance with period-based rankings.
Threats_List: Natural weathering; Climate change; Visitor pressure; Military heritage deterioration
Integrity_Notes: Rock itself permanent; built structures in various states. Archaeological deposits partially investigated.
Comparators: Edinburgh Castle Rock; Stirling Castle; other Scottish fortress-rocks
Unique_Features: Community co-production of significance; rock climbing as heritage value; nature-culture integration; first community-contributed Statement of Significance
